title: How the Chrome DevTools Protocol Works

blocks.text: How the Chrome DevTools Protocol Works
The Chrome DevTools Protocol lets tools instrument, inspect, and control Chromium-based browsers over a WebSocket connection.
Each command targets a domain such as Page, Runtime, or Network, and receives a structured JSON response that the client correlates by id.
A target is a page or worker; attaching to one yields a session id that scopes subsequent commands to that target without disturbing others.
